1 
 
Report: Pre Professional/ Professional Practice 
Facultad de Ingeniería y Ciencias Aplicadas,  
Universidad de los Andes 
 
Sofia Castelli 
4to 
 
Empresas SB Preunic. 
Preunic is a retail chain under Empresas SB, specializing in beauty, personal care and household 
products, with a strong focus on customers satisfaction.  
 
Abstract 
Preunic, a subsidiary of Empresas SB, is a leading beauty, personal care, and household products 
retailer in Chile, with a strong market presence across physical store and online platforms. The 
company is committed to customer satisfaction, competitive pricing, and organizational efficiency, 
leveraging Business Intelligence tools to optimize its decision-making processes.  
During my internship in the BI department, I was responsible for collecting, validating, analyzing, and 
visualizing data, which contributed to strategic decision-making within the organization. My main 
tasks included developing interactive dashboards in Qlik, optimizing SQL queries, generating reports 
for various departments, and conducting market and competitor analysis. Additionally, I collaborated 
with marketing, sales, and finance teams, ensuring that data-driven insights aligned with business 
objectives. Among the most significant challenges I faced was designing a product elasticity 
dashboard, which required analyzing how products performed within Preunic and the broader market. 
Another key challenge was optimizing the “Días de Alcance” query, which calculated stock coverage 
and provided essential inventory planning insights. 
Despite facing challenges related to data validation, panel usability, and market data inconsistencies, 
I successfully addressed them through iterative improvements, methodological refinements, and 
stakeholder-driven adjustments. These experiences allowed me to enhance my technical proficiency 
in SQL, data visualization, and analytical problem-solving, while also strengthening my ability to 
collaborate and adapt within a dynamic business environment. This internship improved my technical 
skills and helped me understand strategic decision-making. I also learned to collaborate effectively 
and the importance of continuous learning—skills that will be key in my career. 
 
 

2 
 
1. Introduction 
1.1 Company Description 
Preunic is one of Chile’s top retailers, specializing in beauty, personal care, and household essentials. 
It also offers toys and stationery. With a history spanning over 70 years, the company has established 
a strong reputation for offering affordable, high-quality products tailored to consumer needs. In 2012, 
Preunic became part of Empresas SB, a major holding company that also owns Salcobrand, one of 
Chile’s largest pharmacy chains. This integration strengthened Preunic’s market presence, allowing it 
to leverage synergies in retail, logistics, and customer experience, reinforcing its commitment to 
operational excellence and customer satisfaction. 
Preunic’s core business revolves around providing a diverse portfolio of beauty and self-care products, 
with a particular emphasis on women’s beauty and wellness. The company offers a broad selection 
of cosmetics, skincare, haircare, fragrances, wellness items, home essentials, and seasonal products. 
To ensure accessibility and convenience, Preunic operates through both a nationwide network of over 
100 physical stores and a robust e-commerce platform, catering to a wide range of customers across 
Chile. 
With a workforce of more than 3,000 employees, Preunic is a key player in the Chilean retail industry, 
supported by the broader success of Empresas SB, which generates millions in annual revenue. The 
company maintains a strong market position, ranking among the top beauty and self-care retailers 
in the country. 
 
To maintain operational efficiency and seamlessly integrate business functions, Preunic follows a 
structured organizational framework. The Executive Management team oversees corporate strategy, 
growth, and expansion, while Sales and Operations handle retail management, inventory control, and 
supply chain logistics. The Marketing department drives branding, customer engagement, and 
promotional campaigns, and Finance & Accounting ensures financial planning, revenue management, 
and budget allocation. The Business Intelligence team plays a crucial role in supporting data-driven 
decision-making, providing analytics and insights that optimize sales performance, inventory levels, 
and customer behavior. Meanwhile, the IT and E-Commerce division manages digital infrastructure, 
online sales, and system integration, enhancing the company’s digital transformation strategy. 

3 
 
Preunic’s ability to integrate retail expertise, data analytics, and customer insights has been 
fundamental to its steady growth and competitive edge. By leveraging technology, strategic 
partnerships, and market intelligence, the company continues to expand its presence, enhance 
customer experience, and drive innovation in the beauty and personal care sector. 
1.2 Department/Area Description 
The Business Intelligence area in Preunic plays a crucial role in leveraging data to support decision-
making across the organization. This department is responsible for collecting, processing, analyzing, 
and visualizing data to provide insight that enhance business strategies and operations. By 
transforming raw data into actionable information, the BI team helps different department optimize 
their processes, improve efficiency, and adapt to market trends.  
Activity Performed:  
• 
Data collections and processing  
• 
Data analysis and reporting 
• 
Performance monitoring  
• 
Market and competitor analysis 
• 
Automation and optimizations of process  
• 
Cross departmental support  
Relationship with other areas:  
The BI department serves as a strategic bridge between data and decision-making, collaborating 
closely with various departments to enhance business operations. It supports the Marketing 
Department by providing insights into customer preferences, purchasing behaviors, and campaign 
performance, enabling the development of data-driven strategies. In Sales and Operations, the BI 
team analyzes sales performance, inventory levels, and operational efficiency, ensuring effective 
supply chain management and demand forecasting. Additionally, it plays a crucial role in the Finance 
Department by delivering accurate financial reports, assisting in planning, forecasting, and 
profitability analysis. Lastly, the BI team works with IT and Data Management to uphold data 
integrity, security, and system integration, ensuring the seamless flow of information across the 
organization. 
Organizational Structure of the BI Department 
 

4 
 
 
The BI department is led by Alberto Pinto, who oversees strategy implementation and ensures that 
the team’s work aligns with Preunic’s business objectives. Reporting to Alberto, Yosybel Olavarría and 
Hugo Riquelme handle various aspects of data processing, visualization, and automation. Additionally, 
Gustavo Majano works as a Data Scientist, analyzing customer behavior, sales trends, and inventory 
data to optimize pricing, marketing strategies, and supply chain efficiency, enabling data-driven 
decision-making. 
Throughout my internship, I worked closely with Alberto, receiving continuous guidance on data 
analysis best practices, dashboard design, and visualization techniques. Their mentorship helped me 
develop a deeper understanding of BI’s impact on business operations, strengthening both my 
technical and analytical skills while reinforcing the importance of data-driven decision-making. 
The BI department at Preunic is a dynamic and essential part of the organization, empowering the 
company with valuable insights that drive efficiency, growth, and adaptability in an ever-evolving 
market. 
 

5 
 
2. Work Details 
2.1 General Activities 
During my internship at Preunic’s BI department, I worked on different projects, including: 
Familiarization with Tools and Training: Initially, I took time to familiarize myself with the tools and 
processes essential for my tasks, such as Qlik and DBeaver. Where I received training with the 
guidance of experience team members, to navigate these systems and understand their impact on 
daily operations. Which not helped me develop technical skills but also gave me a clearer picture of 
how everything connects within the organization. This foundation allowed me to quickly adapt and 
contribute meaningfully to the team.  
Result Validations: On multiple occasions, I performed data validations, updates, and year over year 
comparisons to analyze trends and differences. This activity involved details analysis to identify and 
address any significant discrepancies, ensuring data accuracy and consistency. Using SQL, I was able 
to extract, compare, and refine data, allowing for more reliable insights and supporting better 
decision-making processes.  
Reports and Dashboards Generation:  Throughout my internship, I develop multiples reports for 
different areas within the organization, utilizing SQL queries to extract relevant data and using 
PowerPoint to present insights in a clear and comprehensive manner. Additionally, I designed and 
refined interactive dashboards in Qlik, incorporating charts and tables to enhance user experience 
and facilitate data interpretation. These dashboards were instrumental in analyzing product 
performance, both within Preunic and in the broader market, allowing for better decision-making and 
strategic planning.  
During my internship, I had the opportunity to work in a collaborative and dynamic environment, 
interacting with professionals from different areas, both within and outside the Preunic BI team. 
Talking to different teams helped me understand business needs and improve my reports and 
dashboards so they were useful for the company. 
A significant part of my work involved close collaboration with data analysts and business managers, 
who relied on my report and dashboards for decision-making. By working with them, I gained valuable 
insights into how different teams utilized data to drive strategies and optimize operations. My 
interactions were not limited to internal teams in BI; I also engaged with external stakeholders, such 
as suppliers and market analysts, when validating data related to product performance and market 
trends.  
Additionally, I received continuous feedback from team members such as Hugo and Yosybel, who 
played an important role in guiding me through the technical aspects of Qlik and SQL query 
optimization. Their mentorship helped me refine my analytical skills and improve the efficiency of the 
reports and dashboards I developed.  
Beyond data-related tasks, my role also involved participation in team discussions and strategy 
meetings daily, where I had the chance to contribute ideas and better understand the broader goals 

6 
 
of the company. These experiences reinforced the importance of clear communications and cross-
functional collaboration in ensuring the success of business intelligence initiatives. 
 
 
 

7 
 
2.2 Most Challenge Activity 
One of the most significant challenges I faced during internship was designing and optimizing 
interactive dashboards in Qlik. This required integrating multiple data sources, ensuring accuracy, 
and presenting insights in a way that was both accessible and actionable for users. The complexity 
lay in structuring large volumes of data efficiently while maintaining performance, usability and clarity. 
Another major challenge was creating a price elasticity dashboard to analyze product performance 
both at Preunic and in the market. This dashboard compared internal sales trends with market 
behavior and incorporated “what-if” scenarios to predict future demand based on price adjustments. 
This analysis was crucial for pricing decisions, allowing the company to determinate whether a 
product was inelastic or elastic, guiding strategic pricing policies. To accomplish this, I had to revisit 
economic elasticity concepts, study existing data structure, craft SQL queries and design a user-
friendly dashboard that effectively conveyed insights to decision-making.  
Another significant project involved optimizing a complex query related to “Días de Alcance”, which 
calculates how many days worth of stock is available and when restocking is needed. The original 
query was inefficient, affecting performance and accuracy. After refining the query for better 
execution time and precision, I integrated it into Qlik and built a new dashboard. This dashboard was 
critical for inventory planning, as it provided real-time insights on stock levels and purchasing needs. 
Given its importance, it was essential to ensure the data was 100% accurate and aligned with 
business expectations.  
To tackle these challenges, I worked closely with Hugo, who guided me in mastering Qlik’s 
functionalities, and Yosybel, who helped optimize SQL queries to improve efficiency. The elasticity 
analysis required continuous feedback and adjustments to ensure stakeholders could easily interpret 
the data, leading to multiple iterations where I applied user feedback to improve dashboard clarity. 
Similarly, for “Días de Alcance”, I engaged in multiple meetings to understand the requirements and 
limitations of the existing query, ensuring the final dashboard met all business needs.  
Throughout this process, I collaborated with cross functional teams, to align my work with business 
objectives. Their input was instrumental in refining my dashboards, and by engaging in ongoing 
discussions and iterative improvements, I was able to deliver tolls that significantly enhanced 
decision-making.  
This experience not only strengthened my technical and analytical skills but also reinforced the 
importance of collaboration, adaptability, and clear communication in delivering impactful business 
intelligence solutions.  
 

8 
 
3. Analysis 
3.1 Company Analysis 
Preunic is a leading retail company in Chile, specializing in beauty, personal care, and household 
products. With a strong presence, the company operates both physical store and a web page 
platform, ensuring broad accessibility to costumers. The following is a general analysis of the 
organization, addressing relevant aspects such as productivity, capacity, employees, among others. 
- 
Productivity:  
Productivity relies heavily on data driven strategies and efficient resource management. The company 
leverages BI tools such as Qlik and databases to monitor and optimize key performance indicators ¿, 
including sale trends, inventory levels, and customer behavior. A well-structured supply chain 
prevents stock shortages and overstock situations. Additionally, Preunic actively explores 
sustainability initiatives, such as promoting cruelty-free and eco-friendly products, aligning with 
emerging consumer preferences and industry trends. 
- 
Innovation capacity:  
Preunic demonstrates a strong commitment to innovation, integrating technology and data analytics 
into its business model to enhance decision-making. The company continuously improves its platform, 
allowing for a wider market reach and increased sales. The integration of BI tools and real-time 
monitoring systems optimizes operations, ensuring that strategic decisions are based on accurate, 
data-driven insights.  
- 
Human resources:  
The company employs a diverse workforce, including professionals in business intelligence, sales, 
marketing, operations, and customer service. The company fosters a collaborative work culture, 
where cross-functional teams engage in joint projects to optimize operations and improve customer 
satisfaction. Continuous employee development programs ensure that teams remain equipped with 
the necessary technical and analytical skills to contribute effectively.  
 
Despite its strong market position, Preunic faces challenges such as intense competition, economic 
fluctuations, and shifting consumer behaviors. To remain competitive, the company must continue 
investing in innovation, expanding its digital presence, and refining data driven strategies. 
Additionally, opportunities for international expansion, deeper digitalization, and enhanced Business 
Intelligence capabilities present new avenues for long-term growth, allowing it to strengthen its 
market presence and adapt to evolving industry demands. 
Preunic’s commitment to customer satisfaction, technological integration, and employee development 
positions it well for sustained success. However, to maintain its competitive edge, the company must 
proactively adapt to market shifts, enhance operational efficiency, and leverage emerging 
technologies. By continuing to optimize its processes and embrace innovation, Preunic can reinforce 
its leadership in the Chilean retail industry while positioning itself for future expansion and long-term 
stability. 

9 
 
3.2 Work Analysis 
During my internship with the Business Intelligence team at Preunic, I had the opportunity to 
contribute to several key activities that directly impacted the company’s operations. I provided a 
detailed analysis of the work I performed, the outcomes it produced, the mistakes I encountered, 
and the measures I propose to minimize or solve these issues moving forward. 
Contributions to the Organization 
The BI team at Preunic is responsible for several crucial tasks aimed at optimizing business strategies 
and ensuring informed decision-making across various departments.  
Data collection and processing: One of my primary responsibilities was ensuring the accuracy and 
consistency of data used by the organization. I helped extract and organize data from multiple 
sources, including sales, inventory, and customer transactions using utilizing queries, I was able to 
validate and update data, ensuring that it met the standards required for accurate reporting. This 
process was essential in maintaining clean, consistent, and reliable data, ensuring that key 
performance indicators, such as sales volume and customer preferences, were accurately 
represented. As a result, my work in data processing and validation contributed significantly to the 
integrity of reports and dashboards, leading to a clearer and more accurate understanding of business 
performance. 
Data analysis and reporting: I contributed to the generation of reports and dashboards that 
summarized business performance. This included creating interactive dashboards in Qlik and 
PowerPoint presentations, which were shared with marketing teams and others areas. The reports I 
developed provided critical insights into sales trends and customer behavior, which helped teams 
optimize marketing strategies and sales efforts. For example, a dashboard I created for price elasticity 
analysis allowed the pricing team to make data-driven decisions about adjusting product prices, 
considering both market trends and internal sales data. 
Performance monitoring: I participated in monitoring key performance indicators such as sales, 
customer behavior, and product performance. This data was essential for informing operational 
decisions assessing the effectiveness of ongoing strategies. By analyzing and visualizing this data, I 
helped the team better understand trends and performance patterns, which led to more informed 
decisions regarding inventory management, promotional campaigns, and customer engagement 
strategies.  
Market and competitor analysis: Another critical aspect of my work involved comparing Preunic’s 
performance with broader market trends and competitors. I worked closely with the marketing and 
sales teams to generate reports that identified potential areas for improvement and allowed us to 
adapt our strategies accordingly. This analysis helped Preunic stay competitive, particularly in 
identifying areas where product performance could be improved, leading to better product positioning 
in the marked.  
Automation and optimization: I also assisted in the automation and optimization of data queries and 
processes, for instance, the optimization of a complex query that was used to track days of stock 
coverage. Optimizing this query improved data processing speed and reduced errors, ensuring that 
inventory and purchasing decisions were based on accurate, real-time data. This allowed the team 
to make quicker and more informed decisions about stock levels and restocking. 

10 
 
Cross departmental support: By collaborating with different areas inside the company, I was able to 
create more targeted and relevant reports that supported each team’s unique objectives, ultimately 
leading to better alignment and more effective decision making across the organization. These 
interactions were essential for ensuring that the data presented to these teams was relevant and 
actionable.  
Mistakes and areas for improvement 
While I was able to contribute to the BI teams, I encountered a few challenges that led to mistakes 
and areas for improvement.  
Inaccurate initial data validation process: At the beginning of my internship, I struggled with ensuring 
a thorough data validation process, which led to discrepancies in some reports. The issue arose from 
my limited understanding of the underlying data structures and making assumptions about how 
certain datasets were organized. To prevent similar mistakes in the future, I would take a more 
structured approach to data validation by conducting a more in-depth review of the data sources 
before starting the validation process and working more closely with experienced team members to 
confirm my understanding of the datasets before making modifications.  
Dashboard user experience challenges: When designing dashboards in Qlik, I initially focused more 
on data presentation than usability, which led to layouts that were not as intuitive as they could been. 
As a result, some users found it difficult to navigate and interpret the dashboards without additional 
guidance. This issue arose due to the fast-paced development process, where I did not allocate 
enough time for user testing and feedback before finalizing the dashboards. To ensure that 
dashboards are clear, accessible and user friendly, I would implement a structured user feedback 
process, gathering insights before finalizing anything, conduct usability testing with stakeholders from 
different department to ensure their needs are met.  
Data interpretation discrepancies: During the price elasticity analysis, I encountered a challenge 
where the same product was interpreted differently depending on the market perspective. The data 
showed variations in pricing behavior and demand elasticity between Preunic’s internal sales trends 
and the broader market, leading to inconsistencies in conclusions. To minimize the accuracy and 
consistency of market comparisons, it could develop separate analysis models for Preunic sales data 
and market wide trends, allowing for more precise insights tailored to each scenario, also to provide 
detailed documentation explaining how market conditions affect product demand differently in each 
environment. By adopting these measures, the price elasticity analysis would provide a more 
structured and context aware view, reducing discrepancies and helping others make data driven 
pricing decisions with clearer understanding of market dynamics.  
 
My internship experience with the BI team at Preunic was both professionally enriching and 
intellectually stimulating. The work I performed directly contributed to the organization’s decision-
making processes, ensuring that reports and dashboards provided accurate, data driven insights to 
support business strategies. While I faced several challenges throughout the internship, each obstacle 
served as a valuable learning opportunity, allowing me to refine my technical skills, enhance my 
analytical thinking, and develop a more structured problem-solving mindset. 

11 
 
Through these experiences, I gained a deeper understanding of data validation, query optimization, 
and dashboard development, as well as the importance of clear communication and collaboration in 
a data driven environment. By implementing the solutions and improvements outlined, I believe I 
can continue to contribute more effectively, delivering even higher quality work in future endeavors. 
 
 

12 
 
4. Personal Reflection 
Throughout my internship, I had the opportunity to grow both professionally and personally, gaining 
valuable insights into the role of data driven decision making in a business environment. Through 
data validation, reporting, and dashboard development, I applied my technical skills and improved 
how I communicate insights. Each task challenged me to think critically, problem-solve, and ensure 
that my work directly contributed to the company’s objectives.  
From a professional standpoint, this experience strengthened my proficiency in SQL, Qlik and data 
visualization, as well my ability to optimize queries and enhance reporting processes. I learned how 
to structure and present data in a way that add value to different participant, ensuring that the 
information provided was both accurate and actionable. In addition, by collaborating with marketing, 
analysts, and other team members, I improved my ability to adapt to different perspectives and refine 
my work based on feedback.  
Beyond the technical aspects, this internship also helped me gain valuable personal growth. Engaging 
with professionals from different backgrounds allowed me to build meaningful professional 
relationship, gain new perspectives, and appreciate the role of teamwork in a communication and 
collaboration, not only in delivering accurate mentorship I received from my colleagues played a 
crucial role in my development, reinforcing the value of knowledge sharing and continuous 
improvement.  
Reflecting on my overall experience, this internship has been instrumental in my professional growth, 
equipping me with technical expertise, analytical problem-solving skills, and a strong ability to 
collaborate within a data-driven environment. More importantly, it has reinforced my adaptability, 
resilience, and capacity to contribute meaningfully in professional settings. This experience not only 
expanded my technical knowledge but also taught me about teamwork, constructive feedback, and 
continuous learning—skills that will be essential in my career.

both at Preunic and in the market. This dashboard compared internal sales trends with market 
behavior and incorporated “what-if” scenarios to predict future demand based on price adjustments. 
This analysis was crucial for pricing decisions, allowing the company to determinate whether a 
product was inelastic or elastic, guiding strategic pricing policies. To accomplish this, I had to revisit 
economic elasticity concepts, study existing data structure, craft SQL queries and design a user-
friendly dashboard that effectively conveyed insights to decision-making.  
Another significant project involved optimizing a complex query related to “Días de Alcance”, which 
calculates how many days worth of stock is available and when restocking is needed. The original 
query was inefficient, affecting performance and accuracy. After refining the query for better 
execution time and precision, I integrated it into Qlik and built a new dashboard. This dashboard was 
critical for inventory planning, as it provided real-time insights on stock levels and purchasing needs. 
Given its importance, it was essential to ensure the data was 100% accurate and aligned with 
business expectations.  
To tackle these challenges, I worked closely with Hugo, who guided me in mastering Qlik’s 
functionalities, and Yosybel, who helped optimize SQL queries to improve efficiency. The elasticity 
analysis required continuous feedback and adjustments to ensure stakeholders could easily interpret 
the data, leading to multiple iterations where I applied user feedback to improve dashboard clarity. 
Similarly, for “Días de Alcance”, I engaged in multiple meetings to understand the requirements and 
limitations of the existing query, ensuring the final dashboard met all business needs.  
Throughout this process, I collaborated with cross functional teams, to align my work with business 
objectives. Their input was instrumental in refining my dashboards, and by engaging in ongoing 
discussions and iterative improvements, I was able to deliver tolls that significantly enhanced 
decision-making.  
This experience not only strengthened my technical and analytical skills but also reinforced the 
importance of collaboration, adaptability, and clear communication in delivering impactful business 
intelligence solutions.  
 

8

company_analysis.texto:
3.1 Company Analysis 
Preunic is a leading retail company in Chile, specializing in beauty, personal care, and household 
products. With a strong presence, the company operates both physical store and a web page 
platform, ensuring broad accessibility to costumers. The following is a general analysis of the 
organization, addressing relevant aspects such as productivity, capacity, employees, among others. 
- 
Productivity:  
Productivity relies heavily on data driven strategies and efficient resource management. The company 
leverages BI tools such as Qlik and databases to monitor and optimize key performance indicators ¿, 
including sale trends, inventory levels, and customer behavior. A well-structured supply chain 
prevents stock shortages and overstock situations. Additionally, Preunic actively explores 
sustainability initiatives, such as promoting cruelty-free and eco-friendly products, aligning with 
emerging consumer preferences and industry trends. 
- 
Innovation capacity:  
Preunic demonstrates a strong commitment to innovation, integrating technology and data analytics 
into its business model to enhance decision-making. The company continuously improves its platform, 
allowing for a wider market reach and increased sales. The integration of BI tools and real-time 
monitoring systems optimizes operations, ensuring that strategic decisions are based on accurate, 
data-driven insights.  
- 
Human resources:  
The company employs a diverse workforce, including professionals in business intelligence, sales, 
marketing, operations, and customer service. The company fosters a collaborative work culture, 
where cross-functional teams engage in joint projects to optimize operations and improve customer 
satisfaction. Continuous employee development programs ensure that teams remain equipped with 
the necessary technical and analytical skills to contribute effectively.  
 
Despite its strong market position, Preunic faces challenges such as intense competition, economic 
fluctuations, and shifting consumer behaviors. To remain competitive, the company must continue 
investing in innovation, expanding its digital presence, and refining data driven strategies. 
Additionally, opportunities for international expansion, deeper digitalization, and enhanced Business 
Intelligence capabilities present new avenues for long-term growth, allowing it to strengthen its 
market presence and adapt to evolving industry demands. 
Preunic’s commitment to customer satisfaction, technological integration, and employee development 
positions it well for sustained success. However, to maintain its competitive edge, the company must 
proactively adapt to market shifts, enhance operational efficiency, and leverage emerging 
technologies. By continuing to optimize its processes and embrace innovation, Preunic can reinforce 
its leadership in the Chilean retail industry while positioning itself for future expansion and long-term 
stability.

work_analysis.texto:
3.2 Work Analysis 
During my internship with the Business Intelligence team at Preunic, I had the opportunity to 
contribute to several key activities that directly impacted the company’s operations. I provided a 
detailed analysis of the work I performed, the outcomes it produced, the mistakes I encountered, 
and the measures I propose to minimize or solve these issues moving forward. 
Contributions to the Organization 
The BI team at Preunic is responsible for several crucial tasks aimed at optimizing business strategies 
and ensuring informed decision-making across various departments.  
Data collection and processing: One of my primary responsibilities was ensuring the accuracy and 
consistency of data used by the organization. I helped extract and organize data from multiple 
sources, including sales, inventory, and customer transactions using utilizing queries, I was able to 
validate and update data, ensuring that it met the standards required for accurate reporting. This 
process was essential in maintaining clean, consistent, and reliable data, ensuring that key 
performance indicators, such as sales volume and customer preferences, were accurately 
represented. As a result, my work in data processing and validation contributed significantly to the 
integrity of reports and dashboards, leading to a clearer and more accurate understanding of business 
performance. 
Data analysis and reporting: I contributed to the generation of reports and dashboards that 
summarized business performance. This included creating interactive dashboards in Qlik and 
PowerPoint presentations, which were shared with marketing teams and others areas. The reports I 
developed provided critical insights into sales trends and customer behavior, which helped teams 
optimize marketing strategies and sales efforts. For example, a dashboard I created for price elasticity 
analysis allowed the pricing team to make data-driven decisions about adjusting product prices, 
considering both market trends and internal sales data. 
Performance monitoring: I participated in monitoring key performance indicators such as sales, 
customer behavior, and product performance. This data was essential for informing operational 
decisions assessing the effectiveness of ongoing strategies. By analyzing and visualizing this data, I 
helped the team better understand trends and performance patterns, which led to more informed 
decisions regarding inventory management, promotional campaigns, and customer engagement 
strategies.  
Market and competitor analysis: Another critical aspect of my work involved comparing Preunic’s 
performance with broader market trends and competitors. I worked closely with the marketing and 
sales teams to generate reports that identified potential areas for improvement and allowed us to 
adapt our strategies accordingly. This analysis helped Preunic stay competitive, particularly in 
identifying areas where product performance could be improved, leading to better product positioning 
in the marked.  
Automation and optimization: I also assisted in the automation and optimization of data queries and 
processes, for instance, the optimization of a complex query that was used to track days of stock 
coverage. Optimizing this query improved data processing speed and reduced errors, ensuring that 
inventory and purchasing decisions were based on accurate, real-time data. This allowed the team 
to make quicker and more informed decisions about stock levels and restocking. 

10 
 
Cross departmental support: By collaborating with different areas inside the company, I was able to 
create more targeted and relevant reports that supported each team’s unique objectives, ultimately 
leading to better alignment and more effective decision making across the organization. These 
interactions were essential for ensuring that the data presented to these teams was relevant and 
actionable.  
Mistakes and areas for improvement 
While I was able to contribute to the BI teams, I encountered a few challenges that led to mistakes 
and areas for improvement.  
Inaccurate initial data validation process: At the beginning of my internship, I struggled with ensuring 
a thorough data validation process, which led to discrepancies in some reports. The issue arose from 
my limited understanding of the underlying data structures and making assumptions about how 
certain datasets were organized. To prevent similar mistakes in the future, I would take a more 
structured approach to data validation by conducting a more in-depth review of the data sources 
before starting the validation process and working more closely with experienced team members to 
confirm my understanding of the datasets before making modifications.  
Dashboard user experience challenges: When designing dashboards in Qlik, I initially focused more 
on data presentation than usability, which led to layouts that were not as intuitive as they could been. 
As a result, some users found it difficult to navigate and interpret the dashboards without additional 
guidance. This issue arose due to the fast-paced development process, where I did not allocate 
enough time for user testing and feedback before finalizing the dashboards. To ensure that 
dashboards are clear, accessible and user friendly, I would implement a structured user feedback 
process, gathering insights before finalizing anything, conduct usability testing with stakeholders from 
different department to ensure their needs are met.  
Data interpretation discrepancies: During the price elasticity analysis, I encountered a challenge 
where the same product was interpreted differently depending on the market perspective. The data 
showed variations in pricing behavior and demand elasticity between Preunic’s internal sales trends 
and the broader market, leading to inconsistencies in conclusions. To minimize the accuracy and 
consistency of market comparisons, it could develop separate analysis models for Preunic sales data 
and market wide trends, allowing for more precise insights tailored to each scenario, also to provide 
detailed documentation explaining how market conditions affect product demand differently in each 
environment. By adopting these measures, the price elasticity analysis would provide a more 
structured and context aware view, reducing discrepancies and helping others make data driven 
pricing decisions with clearer understanding of market dynamics.  
 
My internship experience with the BI team at Preunic was both professionally enriching and 
intellectually stimulating. The work I performed directly contributed to the organization’s decision-
making processes, ensuring that reports and dashboards provided accurate, data driven insights to 
support business strategies. While I faced several challenges throughout the internship, each obstacle 
served as a valuable learning opportunity, allowing me to refine my technical skills, enhance my 
analytical thinking, and develop a more structured problem-solving mindset. 

11 
 
Through these experiences, I gained a deeper understanding of data validation, query optimization, 
and dashboard development, as well as the importance of clear communication and collaboration in 
a data driven environment. By implementing the solutions and improvements outlined, I believe I 
can continue to contribute more effectively, delivering even higher quality work in future endeavors. 
 
 

12 
 
4.

The  company is committed to customer satisfaction, competitive pricing, and organizational efficiency,  leveraging Business Intelligence tools to optimize its decision-making processes.

Additionally, I collaborated  with marketing, sales, and finance teams, ensuring that data-driven insights aligned with business  objectives. Among the most significant challenges I faced was designing a product elasticity  dashboard, which required analyzing how products performed within Preunic and the broader market. Another key challenge was optimizing the “Días de Alcance” query, which calculated stock coverage  and provided essential inventory planning insights.

Despite facing challenges related to data validation, panel usability, and market data inconsistencies,  I successfully addressed them through iterative improvements, methodological refinements, and  stakeholder-driven adjustments. These experiences allowed me to enhance my technical proficiency  in SQL, data visualization, and analytical problem-solving, while also strengthening my ability to  collaborate and adapt within a dynamic business environment.

This internship improved my technical  skills and helped me understand strategic decision-making. I also learned to collaborate effectively  and the importance of continuous learning—skills that will be key in my career. 2    1. Introduction.

1.1 Company Description  Preunic is one of Chile’s top retailers, specializing in beauty, personal care, and household essentials. It also offers toys and stationery. With a history spanning over 70 years, the company has established  a strong reputation for offering affordable, high-quality products tailored to consumer needs. In 2012,  Preunic became part of Empresas SB, a major holding company that also owns Salcobrand, one of  Chile’s largest pharmacy chains.

This integration strengthened Preunic’s market presence, allowing it  to leverage synergies in retail, logistics, and customer experience, reinforcing its commitment to  operational excellence and customer satisfaction. Preunic’s core business revolves around providing a diverse portfolio of beauty and self-care products,  with a particular emphasis on women’s beauty and wellness.

The company offers a broad selection  of cosmetics, skincare, haircare, fragrances, wellness items, home essentials, and seasonal products. To ensure accessibility and convenience, Preunic operates through both a nationwide network of over  100 physical stores and a robust e-commerce platform, catering to a wide range of customers across  Chile.

With a workforce of more than 3,000 employees, Preunic is a key player in the Chilean retail industry,  supported by the broader success of Empresas SB, which generates millions in annual revenue. The  company maintains a strong market position, ranking among the top beauty and self-care retailers  in the country. To maintain operational efficiency and seamlessly integrate business functions, Preunic follows a  structured organizational framework.

The Executive Management team oversees corporate strategy,  growth, and expansion, while Sales and Operations handle retail management, inventory control, and  supply chain logistics. The Marketing department drives branding, customer engagement, and  promotional campaigns, and Finance & Accounting ensures financial planning, revenue management,  and budget allocation.

The Business Intelligence team plays a crucial role in supporting data-driven  decision-making, providing analytics and insights that optimize sales performance, inventory levels,  and customer behavior. Meanwhile, the IT and E-Commerce division manages digital infrastructure,  online sales, and system integration, enhancing the company’s digital transformation strategy.

3    Preunic’s ability to integrate retail expertise, data analytics, and customer insights has been  fundamental to its steady growth and competitive edge. By leveraging technology, strategic  partnerships, and market intelligence, the company continues to expand its presence, enhance  customer experience, and drive innovation in the beauty and personal care sector.

1.2 Department/Area Description  The Business Intelligence area in Preunic plays a crucial role in leveraging data to support decision- making across the organization. This department is responsible for collecting, processing, analyzing,  and visualizing data to provide insight that enhance business strategies and operations. By  transforming raw data into actionable information, the BI team helps different department optimize  their processes, improve efficiency, and adapt to market trends.

Activity Performed:   •  Data collections and processing   •  Data analysis and reporting  •  Performance monitoring   •  Market and competitor analysis  •  Automation and optimizations of process   •  Cross departmental support   Relationship with other areas:   The BI department serves as a strategic bridge between data and decision-making, collaborating  closely with various departments to enhance business operations.

It supports the Marketing  Department by providing insights into customer preferences, purchasing behaviors, and campaign  performance, enabling the development of data-driven strategies. In Sales and Operations, the BI  team analyzes sales performance, inventory levels, and operational efficiency, ensuring effective  supply chain management and demand forecasting.

Additionally, it plays a crucial role in the Finance  Department by delivering accurate financial reports, assisting in planning, forecasting, and  profitability analysis. Lastly, the BI team works with IT and Data Management to uphold data  integrity, security, and system integration, ensuring the seamless flow of information across the  organization.

Organizational Structure of the BI Department     4      The BI department is led by Alberto Pinto, who oversees strategy implementation and ensures that  the team’s work aligns with Preunic’s business objectives. Reporting to Alberto, Yosybel Olavarría and  Hugo Riquelme handle various aspects of data processing, visualization, and automation.

Additionally,  Gustavo Majano works as a Data Scientist, analyzing customer behavior, sales trends, and inventory  data to optimize pricing, marketing strategies, and supply chain efficiency, enabling data-driven  decision-making. Throughout my internship, I worked closely with Alberto, receiving continuous guidance on data  analysis best practices, dashboard design, and visualization techniques.

Their mentorship helped me  develop a deeper understanding of BI’s impact on business operations, strengthening both my  technical and analytical skills while reinforcing the importance of data-driven decision-making. The BI department at Preunic is a dynamic and essential part of the organization, empowering the  company with valuable insights that drive efficiency, growth, and adaptability in an ever-evolving  market. 5    2. embeddings_data.texto: Abstract  Preunic, a subsidiary of Empresas SB, is a leading beauty, personal care, and household products  retailer in Chile, with a strong market presence across physical store and online platforms. The  company is committed to customer satisfaction, competitive pricing, and organizational efficiency,  leveraging Business Intelligence tools to optimize its decision-making processes.

During my internship in the BI department, I was responsible for collecting, validating, analyzing, and  visualizing data, which contributed to strategic decision-making within the organization. My main  tasks included developing interactive dashboards in Qlik, optimizing SQL queries, generating reports  for various departments, and conducting market and competitor analysis.

Additionally, I collaborated  with marketing, sales, and finance teams, ensuring that data-driven insights aligned with business  objectives. Among the most significant challenges I faced was designing a product elasticity  dashboard, which required analyzing how products performed within Preunic and the broader market. Another key challenge was optimizing the “Días de Alcance” query, which calculated stock coverage  and provided essential inventory planning insights.

Despite facing challenges related to data validation, panel usability, and market data inconsistencies,  I successfully addressed them through iterative improvements, methodological refinements, and  stakeholder-driven adjustments. These experiences allowed me to enhance my technical proficiency  in SQL, data visualization, and analytical problem-solving, while also strengthening my ability to  collaborate and adapt within a dynamic business environment.

This internship improved my technical  skills and helped me understand strategic decision-making. I also learned to collaborate effectively  and the importance of continuous learning—skills that will be key in my career. 2    1. Introduction.

1.1 Company Description  Preunic is one of Chile’s top retailers, specializing in beauty, personal care, and household essentials. It also offers toys and stationery. With a history spanning over 70 years, the company has established  a strong reputation for offering affordable, high-quality products tailored to consumer needs. In 2012,  Preunic became part of Empresas SB, a major holding company that also owns Salcobrand, one of  Chile’s largest pharmacy chains.

This integration strengthened Preunic’s market presence, allowing it  to leverage synergies in retail, logistics, and customer experience, reinforcing its commitment to  operational excellence and customer satisfaction. Preunic’s core business revolves around providing a diverse portfolio of beauty and self-care products,  with a particular emphasis on women’s beauty and wellness.

The company offers a broad selection  of cosmetics, skincare, haircare, fragrances, wellness items, home essentials, and seasonal products. To ensure accessibility and convenience, Preunic operates through both a nationwide network of over  100 physical stores and a robust e-commerce platform, catering to a wide range of customers across  Chile.

With a workforce of more than 3,000 employees, Preunic is a key player in the Chilean retail industry,  supported by the broader success of Empresas SB, which generates millions in annual revenue. The  company maintains a strong market position, ranking among the top beauty and self-care retailers  in the country. To maintain operational efficiency and seamlessly integrate business functions, Preunic follows a  structured organizational framework.

The Executive Management team oversees corporate strategy,  growth, and expansion, while Sales and Operations handle retail management, inventory control, and  supply chain logistics. The Marketing department drives branding, customer engagement, and  promotional campaigns, and Finance & Accounting ensures financial planning, revenue management,  and budget allocation.

The Business Intelligence team plays a crucial role in supporting data-driven  decision-making, providing analytics and insights that optimize sales performance, inventory levels,  and customer behavior. Meanwhile, the IT and E-Commerce division manages digital infrastructure,  online sales, and system integration, enhancing the company’s digital transformation strategy.

3    Preunic’s ability to integrate retail expertise, data analytics, and customer insights has been  fundamental to its steady growth and competitive edge. By leveraging technology, strategic  partnerships, and market intelligence, the company continues to expand its presence, enhance  customer experience, and drive innovation in the beauty and personal care sector.

1.2 Department/Area Description  The Business Intelligence area in Preunic plays a crucial role in leveraging data to support decision- making across the organization. This department is responsible for collecting, processing, analyzing,  and visualizing data to provide insight that enhance business strategies and operations. By  transforming raw data into actionable information, the BI team helps different department optimize  their processes, improve efficiency, and adapt to market trends.

Activity Performed:   •  Data collections and processing   •  Data analysis and reporting  •  Performance monitoring   •  Market and competitor analysis  •  Automation and optimizations of process   •  Cross departmental support   Relationship with other areas:   The BI department serves as a strategic bridge between data and decision-making, collaborating  closely with various departments to enhance business operations.

It supports the Marketing  Department by providing insights into customer preferences, purchasing behaviors, and campaign  performance, enabling the development of data-driven strategies. In Sales and Operations, the BI  team analyzes sales performance, inventory levels, and operational efficiency, ensuring effective  supply chain management and demand forecasting.

Additionally, it plays a crucial role in the Finance  Department by delivering accurate financial reports, assisting in planning, forecasting, and  profitability analysis. Lastly, the BI team works with IT and Data Management to uphold data  integrity, security, and system integration, ensuring the seamless flow of information across the  organization.

Organizational Structure of the BI Department     4      The BI department is led by Alberto Pinto, who oversees strategy implementation and ensures that  the team’s work aligns with Preunic’s business objectives. Reporting to Alberto, Yosybel Olavarría and  Hugo Riquelme handle various aspects of data processing, visualization, and automation.

Additionally,  Gustavo Majano works as a Data Scientist, analyzing customer behavior, sales trends, and inventory  data to optimize pricing, marketing strategies, and supply chain efficiency, enabling data-driven  decision-making. Throughout my internship, I worked closely with Alberto, receiving continuous guidance on data  analysis best practices, dashboard design, and visualization techniques.

Their mentorship helped me  develop a deeper understanding of BI’s impact on business operations, strengthening both my  technical and analytical skills while reinforcing the importance of data-driven decision-making. The BI department at Preunic is a dynamic and essential part of the organization, empowering the  company with valuable insights that drive efficiency, growth, and adaptability in an ever-evolving  market. 5    2. Work Details.

2.1 General Activities  During my internship at Preunic’s BI department, I worked on different projects, including:  Familiarization with Tools and Training: Initially, I took time to familiarize myself with the tools and  processes essential for my tasks, such as Qlik and DBeaver. Where I received training with the  guidance of experience team members, to navigate these systems and understand their impact on  daily operations.

Which not helped me develop technical skills but also gave me a clearer picture of  how everything connects within the organization. This foundation allowed me to quickly adapt and  contribute meaningfully to the team. Result Validations: On multiple occasions, I performed data validations, updates, and year over year  comparisons to analyze trends and differences.

This activity involved details analysis to identify and  address any significant discrepancies, ensuring data accuracy and consistency. Using SQL, I was able  to extract, compare, and refine data, allowing for more reliable insights and supporting better  decision-making processes.

Reports and Dashboards Generation:  Throughout my internship, I develop multiples reports for  different areas within the organization, utilizing SQL queries to extract relevant data and using  PowerPoint to present insights in a clear and comprehensive manner. Additionally, I designed and  refined interactive dashboards in Qlik, incorporating charts and tables to enhance user experience  and facilitate data interpretation.

These dashboards were instrumental in analyzing product  performance, both within Preunic and in the broader market, allowing for better decision-making and  strategic planning. During my internship, I had the opportunity to work in a collaborative and dynamic environment,  interacting with professionals from different areas, both within and outside the Preunic BI team.

Talking to different teams helped me understand business needs and improve my reports and  dashboards so they were useful for the company. A significant part of my work involved close collaboration with data analysts and business managers,  who relied on my report and dashboards for decision-making. By working with them, I gained valuable  insights into how different teams utilized data to drive strategies and optimize operations.

My  interactions were not limited to internal teams in BI; I also engaged with external stakeholders, such  as suppliers and market analysts, when validating data related to product performance and market  trends. Additionally, I received continuous feedback from team members such as Hugo and Yosybel, who  played an important role in guiding me through the technical aspects of Qlik and SQL query  optimization.

Their mentorship helped me refine my analytical skills and improve the efficiency of the  reports and dashboards I developed. Beyond data-related tasks, my role also involved participation in team discussions and strategy  meetings daily, where I had the chance to contribute ideas and better understand the broader goals   6    of the company.
These experiences reinforced the importance of clear communications and cross- functional collaboration in ensuring the success of business intelligence initiatives. 7.

2.2 Most Challenge Activity  One of the most significant challenges I faced during internship was designing and optimizing  interactive dashboards in Qlik. This required integrating multiple data sources, ensuring accuracy,  and presenting insights in a way that was both accessible and actionable for users. The complexity  lay in structuring large volumes of data efficiently while maintaining performance, usability and clarity.

Another major challenge was creating a price elasticity dashboard to analyze product performance  both at Preunic and in the market. This dashboard compared internal sales trends with market  behavior and incorporated “what-if” scenarios to predict future demand based on price adjustments. This analysis was crucial for pricing decisions, allowing the company to determinate whether a  product was inelastic or elastic, guiding strategic pricing policies.

To accomplish this, I had to revisit  economic elasticity concepts, study existing data structure, craft SQL queries and design a user- friendly dashboard that effectively conveyed insights to decision-making. Another significant project involved optimizing a complex query related to “Días de Alcance”, which  calculates how many days worth of stock is available and when restocking is needed. The original  query was inefficient, affecting performance and accuracy.

After refining the query for better  execution time and precision, I integrated it into Qlik and built a new dashboard. This dashboard was  critical for inventory planning, as it provided real-time insights on stock levels and purchasing needs. Given its importance, it was essential to ensure the data was 100% accurate and aligned with  business expectations.

To tackle these challenges, I worked closely with Hugo, who guided me in mastering Qlik’s  functionalities, and Yosybel, who helped optimize SQL queries to improve efficiency. The elasticity  analysis required continuous feedback and adjustments to ensure stakeholders could easily interpret  the data, leading to multiple iterations where I applied user feedback to improve dashboard clarity.

Similarly, for “Días de Alcance”, I engaged in multiple meetings to understand the requirements and  limitations of the existing query, ensuring the final dashboard met all business needs. Throughout this process, I collaborated with cross functional teams, to align my work with business  objectives. Their input was instrumental in refining my dashboards, and by engaging in ongoing  discussions and iterative improvements, I was able to deliver tolls that significantly enhanced  decision-making.

This experience not only strengthened my technical and analytical skills but also reinforced the  importance of collaboration, adaptability, and clear communication in delivering impactful business  intelligence solutions. 8    3. Analysis.

3.1 Company Analysis  Preunic is a leading retail company in Chile, specializing in beauty, personal care, and household  products. With a strong presence, the company operates both physical store and a web page  platform, ensuring broad accessibility to costumers. The following is a general analysis of the  organization, addressing relevant aspects such as productivity, capacity, employees, among others.

-  Productivity:   Productivity relies heavily on data driven strategies and efficient resource management. The company  leverages BI tools such as Qlik and databases to monitor and optimize key performance indicators ¿,  including sale trends, inventory levels, and customer behavior. A well-structured supply chain  prevents stock shortages and overstock situations.

Additionally, Preunic actively explores  sustainability initiatives, such as promoting cruelty-free and eco-friendly products, aligning with  emerging consumer preferences and industry trends. -  Innovation capacity:   Preunic demonstrates a strong commitment to innovation, integrating technology and data analytics  into its business model to enhance decision-making. The company continuously improves its platform,  allowing for a wider market reach and increased sales.

The integration of BI tools and real-time  monitoring systems optimizes operations, ensuring that strategic decisions are based on accurate,  data-driven insights. -  Human resources:   The company employs a diverse workforce, including professionals in business intelligence, sales,  marketing, operations, and customer service. The company fosters a collaborative work culture,  where cross-functional teams engage in joint projects to optimize operations and improve customer  satisfaction.

Continuous employee development programs ensure that teams remain equipped with  the necessary technical and analytical skills to contribute effectively. Despite its strong market position, Preunic faces challenges such as intense competition, economic  fluctuations, and shifting consumer behaviors. To remain competitive, the company must continue  investing in innovation, expanding its digital presence, and refining data driven strategies.

Additionally, opportunities for international expansion, deeper digitalization, and enhanced Business  Intelligence capabilities present new avenues for long-term growth, allowing it to strengthen its  market presence and adapt to evolving industry demands. Preunic’s commitment to customer satisfaction, technological integration, and employee development  positions it well for sustained success.

However, to maintain its competitive edge, the company must  proactively adapt to market shifts, enhance operational efficiency, and leverage emerging  technologies. By continuing to optimize its processes and embrace innovation, Preunic can reinforce  its leadership in the Chilean retail industry while positioning itself for future expansion and long-term  stability. 9.

3.2 Work Analysis  During my internship with the Business Intelligence team at Preunic, I had the opportunity to  contribute to several key activities that directly impacted the company’s operations. I provided a  detailed analysis of the work I performed, the outcomes it produced, the mistakes I encountered,  and the measures I propose to minimize or solve these issues moving forward.

Contributions to the Organization  The BI team at Preunic is responsible for several crucial tasks aimed at optimizing business strategies  and ensuring informed decision-making across various departments. Data collection and processing: One of my primary responsibilities was ensuring the accuracy and  consistency of data used by the organization.

I helped extract and organize data from multiple  sources, including sales, inventory, and customer transactions using utilizing queries, I was able to  validate and update data, ensuring that it met the standards required for accurate reporting. This  process was essential in maintaining clean, consistent, and reliable data, ensuring that key  performance indicators, such as sales volume and customer preferences, were accurately  represented.

As a result, my work in data processing and validation contributed significantly to the  integrity of reports and dashboards, leading to a clearer and more accurate understanding of business  performance. Data analysis and reporting: I contributed to the generation of reports and dashboards that  summarized business performance. This included creating interactive dashboards in Qlik and  PowerPoint presentations, which were shared with marketing teams and others areas.

The reports I  developed provided critical insights into sales trends and customer behavior, which helped teams  optimize marketing strategies and sales efforts. For example, a dashboard I created for price elasticity  analysis allowed the pricing team to make data-driven decisions about adjusting product prices,  considering both market trends and internal sales data.

Performance monitoring: I participated in monitoring key performance indicators such as sales,  customer behavior, and product performance. This data was essential for informing operational  decisions assessing the effectiveness of ongoing strategies. By analyzing and visualizing this data, I  helped the team better understand trends and performance patterns, which led to more informed  decisions regarding inventory management, promotional campaigns, and customer engagement  strategies.

Market and competitor analysis: Another critical aspect of my work involved comparing Preunic’s  performance with broader market trends and competitors. I worked closely with the marketing and  sales teams to generate reports that identified potential areas for improvement and allowed us to  adapt our strategies accordingly.

This analysis helped Preunic stay competitive, particularly in  identifying areas where product performance could be improved, leading to better product positioning  in the marked. Automation and optimization: I also assisted in the automation and optimization of data queries and  processes, for instance, the optimization of a complex query that was used to track days of stock  coverage.

Optimizing this query improved data processing speed and reduced errors, ensuring that  inventory and purchasing decisions were based on accurate, real-time data. This allowed the team  to make quicker and more informed decisions about stock levels and restocking.

10    Cross departmental support: By collaborating with different areas inside the company, I was able to  create more targeted and relevant reports that supported each team’s unique objectives, ultimately  leading to better alignment and more effective decision making across the organization. These  interactions were essential for ensuring that the data presented to these teams was relevant and  actionable.

Mistakes and areas for improvement  While I was able to contribute to the BI teams, I encountered a few challenges that led to mistakes  and areas for improvement. Inaccurate initial data validation process: At the beginning of my internship, I struggled with ensuring  a thorough data validation process, which led to discrepancies in some reports. The issue arose from  my limited understanding of the underlying data structures and making assumptions about how  certain datasets were organized.

To prevent similar mistakes in the future, I would take a more  structured approach to data validation by conducting a more in-depth review of the data sources  before starting the validation process and working more closely with experienced team members to  confirm my understanding of the datasets before making modifications.

Dashboard user experience challenges: When designing dashboards in Qlik, I initially focused more  on data presentation than usability, which led to layouts that were not as intuitive as they could been. As a result, some users found it difficult to navigate and interpret the dashboards without additional  guidance. This issue arose due to the fast-paced development process, where I did not allocate  enough time for user testing and feedback before finalizing the dashboards.

To ensure that  dashboards are clear, accessible and user friendly, I would implement a structured user feedback  process, gathering insights before finalizing anything, conduct usability testing with stakeholders from  different department to ensure their needs are met. Data interpretation discrepancies: During the price elasticity analysis, I encountered a challenge  where the same product was interpreted differently depending on the market perspective.
The data  showed variations in pricing behavior and demand elasticity between Preunic’s internal sales trends  and the broader market, leading to inconsistencies in conclusions.

To minimize the accuracy and  consistency of market comparisons, it could develop separate analysis models for Preunic sales data  and market wide trends, allowing for more precise insights tailored to each scenario, also to provide  detailed documentation explaining how market conditions affect product demand differently in each  environment.

By adopting these measures, the price elasticity analysis would provide a more  structured and context aware view, reducing discrepancies and helping others make data driven  pricing decisions with clearer understanding of market dynamics. My internship experience with the BI team at Preunic was both professionally enriching and  intellectually stimulating.

The work I performed directly contributed to the organization’s decision- making processes, ensuring that reports and dashboards provided accurate, data driven insights to  support business strategies. While I faced several challenges throughout the internship, each obstacle  served as a valuable learning opportunity, allowing me to refine my technical skills, enhance my  analytical thinking, and develop a more structured problem-solving mindset.

11    Through these experiences, I gained a deeper understanding of data validation, query optimization,  and dashboard development, as well as the importance of clear communication and collaboration in  a data driven environment. By implementing the solutions and improvements outlined, I believe I  can continue to contribute more effectively, delivering even higher quality work in future endeavors. 12    4.

Personal Reflection  Throughout my internship, I had the opportunity to grow both professionally and personally, gaining  valuable insights into the role of data driven decision making in a business environment. Through  data validation, reporting, and dashboard development, I applied my technical skills and improved  how I communicate insights. Each task challenged me to think critically, problem-solve, and ensure  that my work directly contributed to the company’s objectives.

From a professional standpoint, this experience strengthened my proficiency in SQL, Qlik and data  visualization, as well my ability to optimize queries and enhance reporting processes. I learned how  to structure and present data in a way that add value to different participant, ensuring that the  information provided was both accurate and actionable.

In addition, by collaborating with marketing,  analysts, and other team members, I improved my ability to adapt to different perspectives and refine  my work based on feedback. Beyond the technical aspects, this internship also helped me gain valuable personal growth.

Engaging  with professionals from different backgrounds allowed me to build meaningful professional  relationship, gain new perspectives, and appreciate the role of teamwork in a communication and  collaboration, not only in delivering accurate mentorship I received from my colleagues played a  crucial role in my development, reinforcing the value of knowledge sharing and continuous  improvement.

Reflecting on my overall experience, this internship has been instrumental in my professional growth,  equipping me with technical expertise, analytical problem-solving skills, and a strong ability to  collaborate within a data-driven environment. More importantly, it has reinforced my adaptability,  resilience, and capacity to contribute meaningfully in professional settings.
This experience not only  expanded my technical knowledge but also taught me about teamwork, constructive feedback, and  continuous learning—skills that will be essential in my career.
